text.title: AI Failure Mode Taxonomy Procedure
text.summary: Six-step procedure to ship a 12-mode taxonomy with detectors and mitigation links.

step.name: pull-existing
step.input: Last 12 months of incidents, eval categories, alert names.
step.action: Aggregate the open vocabulary used today. Note overlapping synonyms.
step.decision-gate: If <30 incidents exist, defer the taxonomy — not enough data.

step.name: cluster-to-12
step.action: Cluster into 12 semantically-distinct modes. Force-fit edge cases into the closest neighbour; do not invent a 13th.
step.decision-gate: If >1 cluster cannot fit (≥5 incidents) under 12, raise to owner before continuing.

step.name: write-detectors
step.action: Per mode, write a concrete detector. Prefer machine signal (regex, schema check, threshold); accept human-checklist where unavoidable.
step.decision-gate: If a mode has no plausible detector, it is removed (not the taxonomy padded).

step.name: assign-severity
step.action: Score each mode {low, medium, high, critical} against revenue, safety, trust impact. Sign-off by business owner.
step.decision-gate: If all 12 modes scored "high", calibration is broken — revisit rubric.

step.name: link-methodology
step.action: Per mode, link ≥1 methodology that mitigates or evaluates it. If none exists, file a backlog ticket and link "unmitigated".
step.decision-gate: ≥10 of 12 modes must have a real linked methodology; otherwise taxonomy ships but with a known coverage gap noted.

step.name: publish
step.action: Tag v1.0.0; announce in cross-team channel; update incident-template.md + eval-tag schema + alert config to require a mode id.
step.output: v1.0.0 release + CHANGELOG entry + tooling PRs.
step.decision-gate: Quarterly review on the calendar at the moment of publish.
